name: Mailbox Permission Added and Deleted in a Short Period of Time
notes: This rule triggers when a mailbox permission is added and deleted in a short period of time. This could indicate that a user is trying to get access before performing an administrative action such as accessing or deleting information, or create a forwarding rule, before removing their rights to remain undiscovered.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.User Right Assigned, Authentication.User Right Removed
when at least 2 events are seen with the same Target Username (custom) and different Low Level Category in 5 minutes
when the event matches "Target Username" != "Sender" AQL filter query
thresholds: when at least 2 events are seen with the same Target Username (custom) and different Low Level Category in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Mailbox Permission Added and Deleted in a Short Period of Time on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.User Right Assigned, Authentication.User Right Removed
AND when at least 2 events are seen with the same Target Username (custom) and different Low Level Category in 5 minutes
AND when the event matches "Target Username" != "Sender" AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Mailbox Permission Added Followed by Deletion
Event description: Detects when a mailbox permission is added and deleted in a short period of time
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Sender (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
